title: Data Questions, Displays, Summaries, and Responsible Claims · Mixed Practice Return
summary: Investigate service wait times and satisfaction while protecting the context behind the data. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a community-service data story after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Formulate a statistical question that anticipates variability and names a population.
Classify variables and evaluate whether a sample can represent the target population.
Choose tables and graphs whose encodings fit the variable and comparison.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

stimulus.title: Data Claims case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed review case derived from this module: math beginner data statistics. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A strong association proves that one variable caused the other.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Identify misleading scales, omitted groups, confounding, and correlation-causation errors. Artifact under review: a community-service data story.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Data Claims, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Data Claims applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Identify misleading scales, omitted groups, confounding, and correlation-causation errors.
